name: English Muffins 2
recipeInstructions:
1. Oven is not needed. Use a pan or electric griddle heated to 325°F.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/4 cups flour, yeast, sugar, and salt; mix well.
3. Add warm water (125°F or so) and shortening to the flour mixture. Blend at low speed until moistened.
4. Beat for 3 minutes at medium speed. Gradually stir in enough remaining flour to make a firm dough by hand. Knead on a floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes.
5. Place dough in a greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and allow to rise in a warm place until doubled, about 45 minutes.
6. Punch down dough.
7. On a surface sprinkled with cornmeal, roll dough into 1/4-inch thickness. Cut into 3 to 4-inch circles using a biscuit or cookie cutter.
8. Place muffins on ungreased cookie sheets. Cover and allow to rise at room temperature until doubled, about 30 minutes.
9. Bake on a lightly oiled electric griddle or frying pan at 325°F for about 8 minutes on each side, until deep golden brown.
Yield: About 12 muffins.
